Ruiyang Zhang

Master's student at Harbin Institute of Technology, working on UAV visual localization, cross-modal vision-language navigation, and efficient edge deployment. Actively seeking a 2026 Research Internship in 具身智能.

Publications Email GitHub CV

About

I am a Master's student at Harbin Institute of Technology, working on perception and navigation algorithms for UAVs in GPS-denied environments. My research focuses on how aerial agents can localize and understand their surroundings through vision alone — combining cross-modal matching, spatiotemporal constraints, and multi-modal state estimation.

I am also interested in how language and vision can be jointly leveraged to enable more flexible navigation — moving beyond handcrafted rules toward agents that reason about goals in open-world settings.

Affiliation Harbin Institute of Technology
Degree M.Eng · Control Science
Research UAV Navigation · VLN · Edge Deployment

Projects

Real-time Localization for Embodied Agents in Complex Environments Completed

Addressing the UAV localization challenge in GPS-denied environments via multi-modal perception fusion. Proposed an active sensing strategy based on motion trajectory priors, converting global image retrieval into local region prediction — achieving 40× efficiency gain. Built a multi-modal state estimation framework fusing inertial, visual, and satellite map information, deployed on NVIDIA AGX Orin with <5m positioning accuracy.

Visual Localization Multi-modal Fusion NVIDIA AGX Orin GPS-denied Nav
Cross-modal Vision-Language Navigation for UAVs Completed

Built on the OpenFly platform, reproducing baseline VLN methods and establishing training pipelines. Replaced fixed-threshold keyframe selection with cosine similarity between adjacent frames to reduce redundant information. Designed a follow-up experimental plan introducing Mixture-of-Experts to decompose natural language instructions into subtasks.

UAV VLN OpenFly MoE Cross-modal
ASCENT-Improved — Training-free Frontier Scoring Fix Completed

Identified a flaw in ASCENT's (RAL 2026) additive frontier scoring where proximity can dominate semantic relevance. Proposed a multiplicative modulation fix validated on HM3D in Habitat Simulator — entirely training-free, inference-time only.

Habitat Sim BLIP-2 Qwen2.5-7B AWQ / INT4 Object-Goal Nav
